title: Potential UCSF Construction related Traffic Incidents

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Specific to construction activities associated with the UCSF Parnassus Campus construction projects, please provide the following records:
1) A list or records identifying construction contractors and subcontractors working on hauling/delivery/movement of large vehicles at the the Parnassus Campus construction projects. This includes but not limited to general contractors, subcontractors, trucking companies, and hauling companies involved in material transport.
2) Any documents, reports, traffic plans, or logistics planning materials that estimate or describe the expected or actual volume of large construction vehicles (e.g., dump trucks, haul trucks, concrete trucks, delivery trucks) traveling to or from the Parnassus construction sites. 
3) Any internal reports, safety reviews, incident reviews, or investigations conducted by UCSF or its contractors following the pedestrian fatality involving a construction vehicle near the Parnassus construction site in approximately October 2024.
4) Any contracts, agreements, or work orders between UCSF (or its construction management entities) and Costello Trucking, including any agreements involving hauling, trucking, or material transport services connected to the Parnassus construction projects.

If records are maintained under different terminology (for example, traffic management plans, construction logistics plans, safety reviews, or contractor lists), please provide those records as well.

Request Context:
This request relates to public safety concerns surrounding large construction vehicles operating near the UCSF Parnassus construction sites. Two severe traffic incidents involving large trucks have been reported in the area, including a pedestrian fatality in October 2024. Given ongoing neighborhood concerns regarding truck traffic and pedestrian safety, this request seeks to better understand the scale of construction vehicle activity associated with the project and whether any safety reviews or mitigation measures were implemented following the 2024 incident. If any portion of this request can be clarified or narrowed to assist in locating responsive records, please feel free to contact me.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Clinical Trial Transparency Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

U.S. Food and Drug Administration Freedom of Information Staff 10903 New Hampshire Ave Building 71, Room 2217 Silver Spring, MD 20993-0002

Re: FOIA Request – ESS40001 / CLASS Study Records and Related Oversight

Dear FOIA Officer:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. § 552), I request access to all records held by the FDA, and any related oversight documentation maintained by NIH, HHS Office of Inspector General, CDC, and other federal entities, concerning my participation as a subject in the HIV clinical trial:

Sponsor: Glaxo Wellcome (now GlaxoSmithKline)
Protocol Number: ESS40001 (also referenced internally as ESS30001)
Study Name/Nickname: CLASS Study — combination antiretroviral therapy for HIV-1
Study Duration: May 2000 – circa 2006
Known Clinical Sites: Whitman-Walker Health (Washington, DC), Horizon Physicians Group (Knoxville, TN), AIDS Research Consortium of Atlanta (Atlanta, GA)

Records Requested
A. 	IND / FDA Regulatory Files:
Complete IND file for ESS40001/ESS30001, including all protocol versions, amendments, investigator brochures, safety reports, and regulatory correspondence.
Communications between FDA and Sponsor, Applicant, Clinical Investigators, Sub-Investigators, laboratories, or other subcontracted service providers.
Documentation of adverse events, protocol changes, or drug removals.

B. 	Investigators and Clinical Sites:
Complete list of all investigators, sub-investigators, study coordinators, and staff with contact information and institutional affiliation.
List of all clinical sites, laboratories, or subcontracted facilities involved during enrollment, follow-up, or post-study data analysis.

C. 	Institutional Review Board (IRB) Records:
All IRB records for ESS40001 / CLASS Study and any related sub-studies, including approvals, continuing review letters, meeting minutes, correspondence, and audit reports.
Include records from:
Copernicus Group IRB / WCG IRB
Georgetown University IRB
Emory University IRB and Emory University Healthcare, Inc. IRB
Any other independent, commercial, or institutional IRB or ethics committee that reviewed or oversaw the study.
Include documentation of IRB approval of consent forms, amendments, adverse event reporting, and correspondence with investigators or sponsors.

D. 	Human Subject Protection & Federal Oversight:
Records from OHRP, NIH, CDC, HHS OIG concerning ESS40001, any related sub-studies, or monitoring of compliance with 45 CFR Part 46 and 21 CFR Parts 50 and 54.
Records relating to Federal-wide Assurance (FWA) for protection of human subjects, including names of Human Protections Administrators and Signatory Officials.

E. 	Insurance / Coverage / Third-Party Partners (“Breadcrumbs”):
Any records maintained by the agency concerning insurance coverage, premium payments, or verification of healthcare coverage for Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr. in connection with ESS40001 / CLASS Study, HICP, or other federally/state-funded programs. 
Include records submitted by or referencing third-party contractors, payment processors, affiliates, or other entities, including but not limited to Aid Atlanta, Emory University, Emory Healthcare, or Aids Healthcare Foundation. I understand that records maintained solely by private entities may not be subject to FOIA, but request any such records submitted to or held by the agency. 
Records may also exist under my commonly used name, Chuck DeLozier, or any other variant used by staff, contractors, or third-party entities in agency submissions or internal documentation.

F. 	Participant-Specific Data:
Any records referencing Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., including enrollment, consent forms, sample tracking, adverse events, or other study-specific documentation. 
Any records reflecting review, access, or handling of such records by federal employees, contractors, or IRBs. 
Records may also exist under my commonly used name, Chuck DeLozier, or any other variant used by staff, contractors, or third-party entities in agency submissions or internal documentation.

G.	Processing and Format
I request that these records be provided electronically (PDF format) wherever possible.
I am willing to pay reasonable duplication and search fees, but please notify me if fees are expected to exceed $100.
If portions of the records are exempt under FOIA, I request that all reasonably segregable non-exempt material be released.

H.	 Legal References and Justification
This request concerns my personal participation in federally regulated human subject research, and I am exercising my rights under FOIA and applicable federal law.
Applicable regulations include:
21 CFR Part 50 – Protection of Human Subjects
21 CFR Part 54 – Financial Disclosure by Clinical Investigators
45 CFR Part 46 – Protection of Human Research Subjects (Common Rule)
Disclosure of these records is necessary to understand the oversight, consent, and conduct of research in which I was a participant.

Please contact me at if clarification is required.

Sincerely,

 /s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se
1328 Peachtree Street NE
Atlanta, Georgia 30309

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: City of Patterson: Photographs of graffiti uploaded to the City of Patterson website
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All photographs of graffiti uploaded via the city of Patterson's website. The city of Patterson has an online form for reporting graffiti located at:

https://www.pattersonca.gov/FormCenter/Code-Enforcement-11/Reporting-Graffiti-78

This is a request for just the photographs. This is not a request for any other identifying information. I am explicitly not asking for identifying information from the form itself. 

Since the photos are of items in public view, there is a diminished expectation of privacy for the "subject" of the photo.

Time frame for request: January 1st 2023 to March 1st 2026. 

Please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed. There is a public interest in seeing what has been flagged as graffiti by the public to the city of Patterson. 

There is a real and legitimate public interest in understanding the prevalence of graffiti in Patterson, what's reported to the city as graffiti, and in understanding the efficacy of Patterson's graffiti abatement efforts. 

Thank you to the city of Patterson for the city's commitment to transparency, and I look forward to working together to resolving this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Mapping of TTAB and PTAB opinions to United States Patents Quarterly citations

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Mapping of TTAB and PTAB opinions to United States Patents Quarterly citations
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The most current and complete copies of any list or database the USPTO has which relates TTAB and PTAB case numbers and opinions to citations in the United States Patents Quarterly, published by Bloomberg.

For example, section 1201.01 of the Trademark Manual of Examining Procedure cites the following 2017 TTAB opinion: "Moreno v. Pro Boxing Supplies, Inc., 124 USPQ2d 1028, 1036 (TTAB 2017)", i.e., volume 124 of the United States Patents Quarterly, second series, starting at page 1028.

I am looking for a list or database that would relate those USPQ and USPQ2d citations back to the USPTO's own case numbers so that one could look them up in USPTO systems like TTABVUE.

I am not requesting historical copies to the extent they are merely subsets of newer, more complete versions, or copies of the opinions themselves.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ansel Halliburton

title: Public Records Request under the California Public Records Act

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Public Records Request under the California Public Records Act
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Public Records Request under the California Public Records Act

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act (Gov. Code §6250 et seq.), I request records sufficient to show the receipt and administrative processing of materials submitted to the University of California, Office of the President by Young Jin Jung in September 2025.

Two certified mail deliveries were sent to the Office of the President:

1. Tracking number: 9468211105495866736472
   (Government Claim form)

2. Tracking number: 9468211105495863220776
   (Letter-form narrative containing the detailed factual description, damages, and supporting information referenced in the claim)

The Government Claim form referenced the factual description and damages using “see attached,” while the full narrative and supporting details were transmitted in the accompanying letter delivery.

Accordingly, I request records sufficient to show the following:

1. The date the submission associated with tracking number 9468211105495866736472 was received.
2. Whether the materials associated with both certified mail deliveries were processed together as a Government Claim submission.
3. Any claim file number, claim reference number, or administrative identifier assigned to the submission.
4. Any notice of rejection issued regarding the submission, including the date such notice was mailed or transmitted.

This request seeks only administrative records reflecting receipt, logging, routing, and claim-processing status. It does not request privileged communications, investigative materials, or attorney work product.

If any responsive records are withheld, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon and release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions as required under the California Public Records Act.

If the University contends that no responsive records exist, please confirm whether any record exists reflecting receipt, logging, or routing of the above certified mail tracking numbers.

If the records exist in electronic form, I request that they be provided electronically.

Thank you for your time and assistance.


The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jung, Young Jin

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) The total number of exonerations associated with the work of the State of Tennessee Conviction Integrity Unit, as of the date this request is processed, including: 

·                     The total count of exonerations 

·                     The date ranges those exonerations cover. 

 

2) The total number of cases reviewed (or evaluated/screened) by the CIU to generate those exonerations, including: 

·                     The total number of cases reviewed, and   

·                     The definition used by your office for “reviewed” (e.g., intake screening, preliminary review, or full investigation). 

 

To avoid ambiguity, I request existing records that contain these figures, such as annual reports, internal tracking spreadsheets, databases, dashboards, memoranda, or summary reports. If the requested totals are not maintained in a single record, please provide the records your office does maintain that would allow calculation of: 

·                     The number of CIU requests or applications received, 

·                     The number accepted for review, 

·                     The number fully investigated, and 

·                     The number resulting in relief and/or exoneration. 

 

Time period: From the CIU’s inception to the present (or by calendar year through the most recent year available). 

 

Format: I request that responsive records be provided electronically, preferably in CSV or XLSX format, or otherwise as searchable PDF files, via email. 

 

Fees / waiver: If there are any fees for these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. I would also like to request a waiver of all fees as the disclosure of these records is in public interest and will contribute to the public understanding of the State of Tennessee’s use and outcomes of its Conviction Integrity Unit.  

 

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific exemption(s) relied upon and release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions. If another division or office is better suited to respond, please advise or forward this request as appropriate. 

 

Thank you for your time and assistance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with A.Jay Wagner.

communications.subject: Government Records Access and Management Act Request: follett
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Government Records Access and Management Act, I hereby request the following records:

Public reports show that this school district has been subject to book and curriculum challenges:

https://www.inquirer.com/education/radnor-high-school-book-ban-20250304.html

Therefore, I request the following:

Copies of all communications, including but not limited to: emails, texts, AI chatlogs, Microsoft Teams conversations, and any other electronic or digitally-based communications on all DSD communication platforms, related to inputting book ratings into Destiny Follett, including but not limited to communications with or about Brooke Stephens. 

Any materials provided by Brooke Stephens to DSD about training librarians on how to input book ratings into Destiny. 

1. Copies of all communications—including but not limited to emails, text messages, AI chatlogs, and Microsoft Teams conversations—on all District communication platforms related to the inputting of book ratings into the Destiny Follett system. This request specifically includes all such communications sent to, received from, or referencing Brooke Stephens.

2. Copies of any and all materials, guides, or instructional documents provided by Brooke Stephens to the District regarding the training of librarians or staff on the process of inputting book ratings into Destiny Follett.

3. Any records or internal correspondence indicating whether data derived from Destiny Follett was utilized by Brooke Stephens to create the aforementioned training materials.

4. All system logs, audit trails, or internal reports detailing the specific data accessed, exported, printed, or copied by Brooke Stephens. This includes any formal requests or correspondence from the District to Ms. Stephens regarding the deletion of screenshots, videos, notes, or other unauthorized data.

5. Technical records or IP logs indicating the duration and physical location of system access associated with the credentials used by Brooke Stephens, specifically identifying if access occurred at Reading Elementary or via remote/residential networks.

6. All internal incident reports or communications detailing when the District first became aware of potential unauthorized system access or data breaches involving these credentials, and the subsequent timeline of the District’s response.

Please perform this search for records between Jan 1, 2026 and the time this request is processed. This request is not for commercial purposes and will be used in the course of reporting for 404 Media, an independent, journalist-owned news site (www.404media.co).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Claire Woodcock

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into Chicano activist Reies López Tijerina (September 21, 1926 – January 19, 2015). His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his El Paso Times obituary at the following link: https://web.archive.org/web/20150804231645/http://www.elpasotimes.com/news/ci_27347526/reies-lopez-tijerina-nm-chicano-movement-figure-passes

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an investigative journalist who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest for the Daily Beast, the Asia Times, Responsible Statecraft, the National Interest, Reason Magazine, America Magazine and the Armenian Weekly, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

communications.subject: Delaware Freedom of Information Act Request: GsK Request in Delaware
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Delaware Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records from January 1, 2018 through December 31, 2022 reflecting complaints, explanations, analyses, presentations, escalations, appeals or other related correspondence received by the State Employee Benefits Committee and/or the Statewide Benefits Office (SBO) concerning the inclusion, exclusion, removal, or non-coverage of inhaled respiratory drugs on state-managed healthcare plans, pertaining to to any of the following inhaler products: Advair, Trelegy, Spiriva, Stiolto, Symbicort, and/or Bevespi.

This request is seeking the following record types:

-de-identified complaint and/or escalation logs or summaries,

-internal emails, memoranda, slide decks, meeting notes, text messages, or other related communications materials discussing such complaints

-communications with the pharmacy benefit manager (PBM) and/or health insurer that provides a documented explanation for the reason behind an exclusion, non-coverage, or inclusion

-any communications involving disputes of rebate classifications therein

-analyses, presentations, and/or audit documentation addressing the impacts of respiratory drug formulary changes on members or providers

-any correspondence pertaining to the matter of Best Price

-internal audit summaries referencing portfolio or bundled rebate structures

-records referencing ‘portfolio,’ ‘exclusive,’ ‘preferred,’ or ‘rebate guarantee’ in connection to any of the above products

-PBM presentations shared with the Division discussing contracting strategies for any of the above listed inhaler products

-records referencing "portfolio," "exclusive," "preferred," and/or "rebate" in connection with any of the above-listed inhaler products

This request does not seek, by contrast, proprietary pricing terms, rebate amounts, and/or manufacturer contracts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jonathan Rudenberg, a citizen of Delaware, in coordination with Steve Horn.

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To the VA Release of Information Office,

I am requesting a complete copy of all of my medical and health records maintained by the Department of Veterans Affairs concerning me.

I am the individual who is the subject of these records, and I am making this request on my own behalf.

My identifying information is as follows:

Identifying Information:
Full Name: Rosean Barnes
Date of Birth:11.29.1994
Current Address: 6956 Hanover Parkway unit 200
Greenbelt MD 20770
Telephone:2028978873
Email: roseanbarnes@gmail.com

I am requesting the fullest and most complete copy of my VA medical record, including but not limited to:

- all doctors’ notes
- all progress notes
- all nursing notes
- all mental health notes, to the extent releasable
- all medication and prescription records
- all lab results
- all imaging and radiology reports
- all discharge summaries
- all consultation reports
- all diagnoses and treatment plans
- all handwritten notes and scanned paper notes
- all administrative notes associated with my care
- all records maintained in electronic systems and any archived paper files

Please include records from all dates of treatment and from all VA facilities, clinics, hospitals, community based outpatient clinics, and archived records systems reasonably likely to contain my information.

If any portion of my record is maintained separately, scanned as an image, stored in legacy or archived systems, or held as handwritten documentation, I request that those records also be searched and produced.

I request that these records be provided in electronic format if possible. If electronic production is not available for some records, please provide paper copies of the remaining responsive records.

If any portion of the requested records is withheld, please provide all reasonably segregable portions and identify the basis for any withholding.

Please let me know if you need any additional identifying information to process this request.

Sincerely,  
Rosean Barnes  
March 5,2026

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

rosean barnes

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Attendance Program
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting the following records related to the Perfect Attendance Pays program and the district's February 14, 2026 notice regarding fraudulent use of attendance gift cards:
1. The February 14, 2026 notice or communication sent to students and families regarding the investigation of fraudulent use of attendance gift cards, including any attachments or accompanying materials.
2. Any internal policies, procedures, or written guidelines governing the issuance, tracking, and reporting of lost or stolen gift cards under the Perfect Attendance Pays program.
3. Aggregate data (not individual student records) reflecting: the number of gift cards reported lost, stolen, or misused; the total dollar value of cards reported as compromised; and the number of replacement cards issued, for both the 2025 and 2026 program cycles.
4. Any written communications between DPSCD administration and the gift card vendor regarding fraudulent use, card security, or program integrity, from January 1, 2025 to the present.
5. Any written reports, memoranda, or communications from DPSCD Police or the Office of Inspector General to district administration regarding the gift card fraud investigation, to the extent such records are not exempt under MCL 15.243(1)(b).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jaxen Core
